talk_metadata.talk_title: First‑in‑class B7H3‑targeted sialidase for boosting innate and adaptive anti‑tumor immunity
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "we only saw a modest clinical activity and only in the hypersialylated patients"
Speaker said: "we felt that this may be insufficient to sustain immune activation"
Speaker said: "the desialylation was only lasting a couple of days on tumors"

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Transient tumor desialylation lasting only 2–3 days, limiting sustained immune activation

trial_dilution_risk.trial_name_or_nct: First‑in‑human safety lead‑in for B7H3‑targeted sialidase (platinum‑resistant ovarian cancer)
trial_dilution_risk.flawed_enrollment_criteria: Enrolling all platinum‑resistant ovarian cancer patients without pre‑selecting for high hypersialylation levels
trial_dilution_risk.missing_biomarker: Quantitative hypersialylation score as a mandatory inclusion biomarker
trial_dilution_risk.static_vs_dynamic_failure: No on‑treatment biopsies to monitor durability of desialylation

competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Relies on high‑Km sialidase activity; durability limited without targeting arm
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Pursuing broad first‑line monotherapy despite modest single‑agent activity

cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed paired biopsies with 80‑90% reduction of CD163+ M2 macrophages (strong mechanistic effect) but concluded that single‑agent activity was modest and insufficient to move forward

crispro_opportunity.description: Lack of prospective selection of patients with high tumor hypersialylation limits signal detection
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "we only saw a modest clinical activity and only in the hypersialylated patients"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can develop a quantitative hypersialylation signature to enrich for responders in early trials

crispro_opportunity.description: Transient desialylation suggests a need for sustained enzymatic activity or repeat dosing
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "the desialylation was only lasting a couple of days on tumors"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can model optimal dosing schedules and predict PK/PD windows for durable target engagement

cited_competitors.name: PD‑1/PD‑L1 checkpoint inhibitors
cited_competitors.context: Compared mechanistic distinctness and similar preclinical activity

unresolved_questions: What is the optimal dosing frequency to maintain tumor desialylation beyond 2–3 days?
Can combination with checkpoint blockade overcome the modest single‑agent activity?

assets_to_track: Palleon B7H3‑targeted sialidase (E602 and next‑gen molecule)

talk_metadata.talk_title: ECI‑830, a potent and highly selective CDK2 inhibitor for HR‑positive/HER2‑negative breast cancer and CCNE1‑amplified tumors
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "we observed a shift when you go from biochemical to cell… still very potent"
Speaker said: "the addition of ECI‑830 to ribociclib further suppresses phosphorylated Rb"

vulnerability_identified.failing_compound_or_target: ECI‑830 monotherapy in HR‑positive breast cancer
vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: CDK2 inhibition alone may not fully suppress Rb phosphorylation in RB‑intact tumors

trial_dilution_risk.trial_name_or_nct: Phase 1 study of ECI‑830 (single agent or in combination)
trial_dilution_risk.target_biology: CDK2‑driven cell‑cycle progression in HR‑positive breast cancer and CCNE1‑amplified tumors
trial_dilution_risk.flawed_enrollment_criteria: Potentially enrolling patients without biomarker (CCNE1 amplification or high cyclin E) enrichment
trial_dilution_risk.missing_biomarker: CCNE1 amplification status or cyclin E expression as a mandatory inclusion criterion
trial_dilution_risk.statistical_concern: Early‑phase dose‑escalation may be underpowered to detect modest efficacy signals

competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Selectivity relies on weak multiple interactions in the lower hinge; may be vulnerable to resistance mutations in CDK2
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Combination with ribociclib may dilute differentiation advantage if safety window is narrow

cognitive_dissonance: Speaker presented crystal structure showing high selectivity for CDK2, yet acknowledged that CDK2 inhibition alone has limited single‑agent activity in HR‑positive breast cancer

crispro_opportunity.description: Need for robust CCNE1 amplification or cyclin E expression assay to enrich for responders
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "tumors that have relatively high expression of cyclin E… do benefit… but PFS benefit is less"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can develop a predictive gene‑expression signature for cyclin E‑driven CDK2 dependence

crispro_opportunity.description: Potential to pair ECI‑830 with agents that target adaptive resistance pathways beyond CDK4/6
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "the addition of ECI‑830 to ribociclib further suppresses phosphorylated Rb"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can simulate multi‑target network effects to prioritize synergistic combos

cited_competitors.name: Ribociclib
cited_competitors.context: Standard of care CDK4/6 inhibitor used in combination studies

cited_competitors.name: CDK4/6 inhibitors (class)
cited_competitors.context: Discussed as existing therapy with resistance mechanisms

unresolved_questions: Will CDK2 inhibition overcome resistance in RB‑loss tumors?
What is the safety profile of combining ECI‑830 with ribociclib at therapeutic doses?

talk_metadata.talk_title: TRI‑611, a potent selective CNS‑penetrant ALK molecular glue degrader for ALK‑fusion positive NSCLC
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "TRI‑611 induces degradation of full‑length endogenous EML4‑ALK"
Speaker said: "TRI‑611 is active against all of the mutations that we have put into this system"

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Resistance mutations cluster in the ATP‑binding pocket, leaving distal degrader site unexploited

trial_dilution_risk.target_biology: Cereblon‑mediated degradation of ALK fusion proteins
trial_dilution_risk.flawed_enrollment_criteria: Enrolling TKI‑experienced patients without stratifying for specific resistance mutations

competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Relies on a non‑G‑loop degron; other companies could discover alternative cereblon binders for ALK
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Monotherapy focus in TKI‑experienced population may limit differentiation if combination benefits are not demonstrated

cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed that TRI‑611 degrades ALK in vitro and in vivo, yet concluded that the safety profile is “remarkably clean” without presenting any preclinical toxicity data

key_data_gaps: No human safety or PK data disclosed
Limited data on activity against rare ALK fusion partners

crispro_opportunity.description: TRI‑611 can be positioned to replace failed ALK TKIs in patients with resistance mutations
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "TRI‑611 is active against all of the mutations that we have put into this system"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can model degradation efficiency across the spectrum of ALK resistance mutations to prioritize patient subsets

crispro_opportunity.description: Need for prospective selection of patients harboring ALK resistance mutations for trial enrollment
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "we used the BAF‑3 system to explore activity against known and predicted ALK TKI resistance mutations"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can integrate genomic profiling to generate a resistance‑mutation panel for enrichment

cited_competitors.name: Lorlatinib
cited_competitors.context: Compared activity against TKI‑resistant ALK mutants; lorlatinib lost potency while TRI‑611 retained activity

unresolved_questions: Will TRI‑611 retain activity against future, as‑yet‑unknown ALK resistance mutations?
What is the CNS safety profile given ALK expression in the adult brain is low but not absent?

talk_metadata.talk_title: Discovery and development of a CD30 dual‑payload ADC combining MMAE and TOP1 inhibition
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "our dual payload ADC was tolerated at 60 mg/kg, far above the 10 mg/kg dose of brentuximab vedotin"
Speaker said: "the dual payload ADC showed robust tumor regression at a sub‑optimal dose where single‑payload ADCs did not"
Speaker said: "the chemistry is highly stable, resulting in very little payload loss"

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Efflux of MMAE via P‑gp reduces intracellular toxin concentration
vulnerability_identified.ghost_responder_admission: some patients respond while others are resistant due to P‑gp expression

trial_dilution_risk.trial_name_or_nct: Phase 1 study of the CD30 dual‑payload ADC (internal)
trial_dilution_risk.target_biology: CD30‑mediated delivery of MMAE and TOP1 inhibitor
trial_dilution_risk.flawed_enrollment_criteria: Potentially enrolling CD30‑positive patients without assessing P‑gp expression or tumor heterogeneity
trial_dilution_risk.statistical_concern: Dose‑finding may be confounded by dual payload PK interactions

competitive_moat_weakness.vulnerable_ip_or_chemistry: Non‑natural amino acid site‑specific conjugation platform
competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Relies on a proprietary lysine‑spirocyclopentadiene chemistry; competitors could develop alternative orthogonal chemistries
competitive_moat_weakness.scale_or_manufacturing_bottleneck: Complex stepwise conjugation may affect manufacturing throughput
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Dual payload may complicate regulatory path and safety assessment

cognitive_dissonance: Speaker demonstrated that the dual‑payload ADC has markedly improved tolerability (MTD 60 mg/kg) yet attributed the safety gain primarily to the stable chemistry rather than the reduced MMAE DAR, despite presenting data showing lower MMAE load

key_data_gaps: No human safety or PK data disclosed
Limited data on long‑term immunogenicity of the non‑natural amino acid

crispro_opportunity.description: Identify synergistic agents that further overcome P‑gp mediated resistance when combined with the dual‑payload ADC
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "we retained activity in P‑gp overexpressing resistant models"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can screen for small molecules that down‑regulate MDR1 expression to augment ADC efficacy

crispro_opportunity.description: Incorporate P‑gp expression as a stratification biomarker in early‑phase trials
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "the dual payload ADC showed activity where MMAE‑only ADC failed due to P‑gp"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can develop a predictive model linking MDR1 levels to expected ADC response

cited_competitors.name: Brentuximab vedotin
cited_competitors.context: Benchmark ADC with stochastic DAR4 MMAE conjugation

cited_competitors.name: Other CD30 ADCs (generic)
cited_competitors.context: Mentioned as existing therapies with resistance issues

unresolved_questions: What is the maximum tolerated dose in humans for the dual‑payload construct?
Will the stable chemistry prevent off‑target release of MMAE in non‑tumor tissues?
